prompt:
Goal: Create a warm, realistic premium Japanese café dessert advertisement scene for a seasonal kakigori menu, with a romantic cottagecore flower-garden atmosphere.

Canvas: Vertical 3:4 composition, photorealistic but softly idealized, golden-hour sunlight, shallow depth of field, creamy beige and honey tones, high detail, no harsh contrast.

Layout: Foreground center shows one large ornate glass bowl of kakigori on a matching saucer. Left side contains exactly 3 separate signage elements: 1 tall framed café sign at upper left, 1 framed menu board at lower left, and 1 small paper tag near the bottom left. Right side contains 1 seated young woman behind the dessert and 1 small white rabbit figurine or plush beside the bowl. The background is a rustic wooden café interior with a sunlit window, trailing greenery, lace, dried flowers, and small daisies.

Main subject: A luxurious mound of milk kakigori in a scalloped vintage glass bowl, topped with a round scoop of pale milk ice cream. Decorate the shaved ice with translucent honey syrup, small pale yellow honey jelly cubes, peach-pink fruit pieces, white edible flowers, yellow daisy centers, mint leaves, and glittering ice crystals. The flavor should feel like honey, milk, edible flowers, vanilla, and gentle citrus. Make the dessert look premium, delicate, and refreshing.

Character: Place one young blonde woman in a cream lace cardigan or crochet blouse, holding a gold spoon in her right hand and resting her chin on her left hand. She wears a flower crown of small white, cream, and pale pink blossoms. Her face is intentionally covered by a flat opaque square in warm beige, matching the visible generated image. Use long wavy blonde hair and cream vintage lace cardigan with crochet cuffs.

Text and signage: Upper framed sign should read “CAFÉ Fraise Étoilée” with a smaller Japanese subtitle and the English phrase “Blooming with my own light.” Lower framed menu board should feature a seasonal limited kakigori title in Japanese: 花冠はちみつのミルクかき氷, a small subtitle, several short Japanese ingredient lines, and a large price ¥2,200(税込). The small bottom tag should read “Blooming with my own light.” Add a tiny heart doodle and a small gold wax seal near the tag. Keep the Japanese typography elegant, centered, and café-menu-like.

Visible counted details to include: exactly 1 kakigori bowl, 1 spoon in the woman’s hand, 1 flower crown, 1 white rabbit, 3 signage elements, many scattered daisies and dried blossoms, 1 gold wax seal, and 1 small heart doodle.

Style constraints: Use realistic food photography lighting blended with fairytale café styling. Include lace tablecloth texture, antique frames, pressed-flower ornamentation, transparent glass reflections, warm backlight through the window, and soft bokeh. Avoid modern plastic objects, neon colors, hard shadows, extra people, watermarks, or unrelated text.
